Corollary 1.5$\mathit{I f}\:\sigma>1$ and t is real, then
\operatorname{R e}(n^{-s})=\operatorname{R e}(e^{-(\sigma+i t)\log n})=e^{-\sigma\log n}\cos(t\log n)=n^{-\sigma}\cos(t\log n).

\begin{aligned}{}&{{}\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta^{3}(\sigma)\zeta^{4}(\sigma+i t)\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|}\\ {={}}&{{}3\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma)|+4\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma+i t)|+\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|}\\ {={}}&{{}3{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma)]+4{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma+i t)]+{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma+2i t)]}\\ {={}}&{{}\sum c_{n}n^{-\sigma}(3+4\operatorname{c o s}\theta_{n}+\operatorname{c o s}2\theta_{n})\thinspace,}\\ \end{aligned}
where $\theta_{n}=t\log n$ . The positivity now follows from Lemma 1.4, and the fact that $c_{n}\geq0$

core_content.markdown:
which holds for $0\leq x<1$ , we find that 

$$\log\zeta(s)=\log\prod_{p}{\frac{1}{1-p^{-s}}}=\sum_{p}\log\left({\frac{1}{1-p^{-s}}}\right)=\sum_{p,m}{\frac{p^{-m s}}{m}}.$$

Since the doubl sumconvergesabsolutely,weneed not specifytheorder of summation..Seethe Note attheendof this chapter.The formula then holdsfor all $\mathrm{R e}(s)>1$ by analytic continuation.Note that, by Theorem 6.2 in Chapter 3, log $\zeta(s)$ is well defined in the simply connected half-plane $\mathrm{R e}(s)>1$ ,since ζ has no zeros there. Finally, it is clear that we have 



$$\sum_{p,m}{\frac{p^{-m s}}{m}}=\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}c_{n}n^{-s},$$

where $c_{n}=1/m{\mathrm{~i f~}}n=p^{m}{\mathrm{~a n d~}}c_{n}=0$ otherwise.

The proof of the theorem we shall give depends on a simple trick that is based on the following inequality.



### Lemma 1.4 If $\theta\in\mathbb{R}$ ,then $3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta\geq0$ 

This follows at once from the simple observation 

$$3+4\cos\theta+\cos2\theta=2(1+\cos\theta)^{2}.$$

Corollary 1.5$\mathit{I f}\:\sigma>1$ and t is real, then 

$$\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta^{3}(\sigma)\zeta^{4}(\sigma+i t)\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|\geq0.$$

Proof.Let $s=\sigma+i t$ and note that 

$$\operatorname{R e}(n^{-s})=\operatorname{R e}(e^{-(\sigma+i t)\log n})=e^{-\sigma\log n}\cos(t\log n)=n^{-\sigma}\cos(t\log n).$$

Therefore,

$$\begin{aligned}{}&{{}\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta^{3}(\sigma)\zeta^{4}(\sigma+i t)\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|}\\ {={}}&{{}3\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma)|+4\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma+i t)|+\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|}\\ {={}}&{{}3{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma)]+4{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma+i t)]+{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma+2i t)]}\\ {={}}&{{}\sum c_{n}n^{-\sigma}(3+4\operatorname{c o s}\theta_{n}+\operatorname{c o s}2\theta_{n})\thinspace,}\\ \end{aligned}$$

where $\theta_{n}=t\log n$ . The positivity now follows from Lemma 1.4, and the fact that $c_{n}\geq0$

formulas.rec_formula: \begin{aligned}{}&{{}\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta^{3}(\sigma)\zeta^{4}(\sigma+i t)\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|}\\ {={}}&{{}3\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma)|+4\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma+i t)|+\operatorname{l o g}|\zeta(\sigma+2i t)|}\\ {={}}&{{}3{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma)]+4{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma+i t)]+{\operatorname{R e}}[\operatorname{l o g}\zeta(\sigma+2i t)]}\\ {={}}&{{}\sum c_{n}n^{-\sigma}(3+4\operatorname{c o s}\theta_{n}+\operatorname{c o s}2\theta_{n})\thinspace,}\\ \end{aligned}
\operatorname{R e}(n^{-s})=\operatorname{R e}(e^{-(\sigma+i t)\log n})=e^{-\sigma\log n}\cos(t\log n)=n^{-\sigma}\cos(t\log n).
